First, address the high-severity session inactivity bug by ensuring the session cookie's maxAge is explicitly refreshed on every authenticated request; currently, the server-side session slides, but the non-sliding client cookie expires prematurely, violating the 30-minute inactivity requirement. Second, fix the data scan failure in the group-buy repository that occurs after a user is deleted: update the domain model and repository logic to handle a nullable OrganizerID (e.g., using *uuid.UUID or sql.NullUUID), aligning the code with the ON DELETE SET NULL database migration. While working in the core API layer, also harden the application's security posture by moving admin authorization checks out of individual handlers and into a centralized RequireAdmin middleware applied directly to the /api/admin route group.

Additionally, correct the remaining business logic, presentation, and documentation gaps to fully align with the product specifications. Modify the resource capacity calculations to explicitly exclude "waitlisted" bookings from the active seat consumption queries, ensuring they do not artificially deplete available inventory. In the frontend Templ views, implement a presentation mapping function to convert raw technical database enums (like pending_confirmation) into human-readable UI labels (e.g., "Pending Confirmation"). Finally, update the architecture section of the README.md to accurately list all active migration files (including 0003 and 0004), and expand the test suite to explicitly cover the new cookie-sliding semantics and verify that group-buy records can be successfully read after an organizer is deleted.

16	// Deps groups every dependency the router needs. main.go is the only place
17	// that constructs this struct, keeping wiring explicit.
18	type Deps struct {
19		Logger *slog.Logger
20		Auth   *service.AuthService
21	
22		Cache       *cache.Cache
23		Idempotency repository.IdempotencyRepository
24	
25		HealthHandler       *handlers.HealthHandler

27		ResourceHandler     *handlers.ResourceHandler

29		GroupHandler        *handlers.GroupHandler
30		GroupBuyHandler     *handlers.GroupBuyHandler

32		NotificationHandler *handlers.NotificationHandler
33		AnalyticsHandler    *handlers.AnalyticsHandler

36	}

38	func NewRouter(d Deps) http.Handler {
39		gin.SetMode(gin.ReleaseMode)
40	
41		r := gin.New()
42		r.Use(middleware.Recovery(d.Logger))
43		r.Use(middleware.RequestLogger(d.Logger))
44	
45		soft := middleware.Authenticator(d.Auth, false)
46		must := middleware.Authenticator(d.Auth, true)
47		notBlacklisted := middleware.RequireNotBlacklisted()
48		rotated := middleware.RequirePasswordRotated()
49		idem := middleware.Idempotency(d.Idempotency)
50		cacheMW := middleware.ReadThroughCache(d.Cache)
51	
52		// Health
53		r.GET("/healthz", d.HealthHandler.Liveness)
54		r.GET("/readyz", d.HealthHandler.Readiness)
55	
56		// HTML pages
57		r.GET("/", soft, d.BookingHandler.Index)
58		r.GET("/auth/login", d.AuthHandler.LoginPage)
59		r.GET("/auth/register", d.AuthHandler.RegisterPage)
60		r.GET("/availability", soft, d.ResourceHandler.AvailabilityPage)
61		r.GET("/bookings/new", must, rotated, notBlacklisted, d.BookingHandler.NewPage)
62		r.GET("/groups", soft, d.GroupHandler.IndexHTML)
63		r.GET("/groups/:id", soft, d.GroupHandler.DetailHTML)
64		r.GET("/group-buys", soft, d.GroupBuyHandler.IndexHTML)
65		r.GET("/group-buys/:id", soft, d.GroupBuyHandler.DetailHTML)
66		r.GET("/documents", must, rotated, d.DocumentHandler.CenterHTML)
67		r.GET("/notifications", must, rotated, d.NotificationHandler.CenterHTML)
68		r.GET("/admin/analytics", must, rotated, d.AnalyticsHandler.DashboardHTML)
69	
70		// JSON API
71		api := r.Group("/api")
72		{
73			// --- auth (public) ---
74			auth := api.Group("/auth")
75			{
76				auth.POST("/register", d.AuthHandler.Register)
77				auth.POST("/login", d.AuthHandler.Login)
78				auth.POST("/logout", soft, d.AuthHandler.Logout)
79				auth.GET("/me", must, d.AuthHandler.Me)
80				auth.GET("/captcha", d.AuthHandler.Captcha)
81				// change-password is intentionally NOT behind `rotated` so users
82				// flagged for rotation can use it; the gate covers everything
83				// else.
84				auth.POST("/change-password", must, d.AuthHandler.ChangePassword)
85			}
86	
87			// --- read-only resource catalog (cached) ---
88			api.GET("/resources", soft, cacheMW, d.ResourceHandler.List)
89			api.GET("/resources/:id/remaining", soft, d.ResourceHandler.RemainingSeats)
90			api.GET("/availability", soft, cacheMW, d.ResourceHandler.Availability)
91	
92			// --- bookings ---
93			bookings := api.Group("/bookings", must, rotated)
94			{
95				bookings.POST("", notBlacklisted, idem, d.BookingHandler.Create)
96				bookings.GET("", d.BookingHandler.ListMine)
97				bookings.GET("/:id", d.BookingHandler.Get)
98				bookings.POST("/:id/transition", idem, d.BookingHandler.Transition)
99			}
100	
101			// --- group reservations ---
102			groups := api.Group("/groups")
103			{
104				// Create requires a session so the organiser-of-record is known
105				// at insert time; the masking gate uses organizer_id to decide
106				// whether to leak the email and full name on shared views.
107				groups.POST("", must, rotated, d.GroupHandler.Create)
108				groups.GET("", soft, cacheMW, d.GroupHandler.List)
109				groups.GET("/:id", soft, d.GroupHandler.Get)
110			}
111	
112			// --- GROUP BUYS ---
113			gb := api.Group("/group-buys")
114			{
115				gb.POST("", must, rotated, notBlacklisted, idem, d.GroupBuyHandler.Create)
116				gb.GET("", soft, cacheMW, d.GroupBuyHandler.List)
117				gb.GET("/:id", soft, d.GroupBuyHandler.Get)
118				gb.GET("/:id/progress", soft, d.GroupBuyHandler.Progress)
119				gb.GET("/:id/participants", soft, d.GroupBuyHandler.Participants)
120				gb.POST("/:id/join", must, rotated, notBlacklisted, idem, d.GroupBuyHandler.Join)
121			}
122	
123			// --- DOCUMENTS ---
124			docs := api.Group("/documents", must, rotated)
125			{
126				docs.POST("/confirmation", idem, d.DocumentHandler.Confirmation)
127				docs.POST("/checkin-pass", idem, d.DocumentHandler.CheckinPass)
128				docs.GET("", d.DocumentHandler.List)
129				docs.GET("/:id", d.DocumentHandler.Get)
130				docs.GET("/:id/content", d.DocumentHandler.Content)
131			}
132	
133			// --- NOTIFICATIONS & TODOS ---
134			notify := api.Group("", must, rotated)
135			{
136				notify.GET("/notifications", d.NotificationHandler.List)
137				notify.GET("/notifications/unread-count", d.NotificationHandler.UnreadCount)
138				notify.POST("/notifications/:id/read", d.NotificationHandler.MarkRead)
139	
140				notify.POST("/todos", d.NotificationHandler.CreateTodo)
141				notify.GET("/todos", d.NotificationHandler.ListTodos)
142				notify.POST("/todos/:id/status", d.NotificationHandler.UpdateTodoStatus)
143			}
144	
145			// --- ANALYTICS ---
146			api.POST("/analytics/track", soft, d.AnalyticsHandler.Track)
147			api.GET("/analytics/top", soft, cacheMW, d.AnalyticsHandler.Top)
148			api.GET("/analytics/trends", soft, cacheMW, d.AnalyticsHandler.Trends)
149	
150			// --- GOVERNANCE ---
151			api.GET("/governance/dictionary", soft, cacheMW, d.GovernanceHandler.Dictionary)
152			api.GET("/governance/tags", soft, cacheMW, d.GovernanceHandler.Tags)
153			api.POST("/consent/grant", must, rotated, d.GovernanceHandler.GrantConsent)
154			api.POST("/consent/withdraw", must, rotated, d.GovernanceHandler.WithdrawConsent)
155			api.GET("/consent", must, rotated, d.GovernanceHandler.ListConsent)
156			api.POST("/account/delete", must, rotated, d.GovernanceHandler.RequestDeletion)
157			api.POST("/account/delete/cancel", must, rotated, d.GovernanceHandler.CancelDeletion)
158	
159			// --- ADMIN ---
160			admin := api.Group("/admin", must, rotated)
161			{
162				admin.GET("/notification-deliveries", d.NotificationHandler.AdminDeliveries)
163				admin.GET("/anomalies", d.AnalyticsHandler.Anomalies)
164				admin.POST("/import/resources", d.GovernanceHandler.ImportResources)
165				admin.GET("/export/resources.csv", d.GovernanceHandler.ExportResources)
166				admin.GET("/cache/stats", d.AdminHandler.CacheStats)

170				admin.POST("/webhooks/:id/disable", d.AdminHandler.WebhookDisable)

17	type AuthHandler struct {
18		auth *service.AuthService
19		log  *slog.Logger
20	}
21	
22	func NewAuthHandler(auth *service.AuthService, log *slog.Logger) *AuthHandler {
23		return &AuthHandler{auth: auth, log: log}
24	}
25	
26	// ---------- requests ----------
27	
28	type registerRequest struct {
29		Username string `json:"username" form:"username" binding:"required"`
30		Password string `json:"password" form:"password" binding:"required"`
31	}
32	
33	type loginRequest struct {
34		Username      string `json:"username"       form:"username"       binding:"required"`
35		Password      string `json:"password"       form:"password"       binding:"required"`
36		CaptchaToken  string `json:"captcha_token"  form:"captcha_token"`
37		CaptchaAnswer string `json:"captcha_answer" form:"captcha_answer"`
38	}
39	
40	type changePasswordRequest struct {
41		CurrentPassword string `json:"current_password" form:"current_password" binding:"required"`
42		NewPassword     string `json:"new_password"     form:"new_password"     binding:"required"`
43	}
44	
45	// ---------- JSON endpoints ----------
46	
47	func (h *AuthHandler) Register(c *gin.Context) {
48		var req registerRequest
49		if err := c.ShouldBind(&req); err != nil {
50			c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
51			return
52		}
53		user, err := h.auth.Register(c.Request.Context(), req.Username, req.Password)
54		if err != nil {
55			writeAuthError(c, h.auth, err)
56			return
57		}
58		c.JSON(http.StatusCreated, user)
59	}
60	
61	func (h *AuthHandler) Login(c *gin.Context) {
62		var req loginRequest
63		if err := c.ShouldBind(&req); err != nil {
64			c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
65			return
66		}
67		res, err := h.auth.Login(c.Request.Context(), service.LoginInput{
68			Username:      req.Username,
69			Password:      req.Password,
70			CaptchaToken:  req.CaptchaToken,
71			CaptchaAnswer: req.CaptchaAnswer,
72			UserAgent:     c.Request.UserAgent(),
73			IP:            c.ClientIP(),
74		})
75		if err != nil {
76			writeAuthError(c, h.auth, err)
77			return
78		}
79	
80		settings := h.auth.Settings()
81		maxAge := int(settings.SessionInactivity.Seconds())

136	// ComputeSlotCapacities slices [open, close) into fixed-width slots and, for
137	// each slot, computes:
138	//   - capacity        = the resource's total seat budget
139	//   - active_party    = sum of party_size for bookings that overlap the slot
140	//                       and are in an active state

142	//
143	// It is intentionally side-effect free so unit tests can exercise every
144	// branch (no overlap, partial overlap, full saturation, oversold edge).
145	func ComputeSlotCapacities(open, close time.Time, slot time.Duration, capacity int, booked []domain.Booking) []SlotCapacity {
146		if !close.After(open) || slot <= 0 || capacity < 0 {
147			return nil
148		}
149		out := make([]SlotCapacity, 0, int(close.Sub(open)/slot))
150		for cur := open; cur.Before(close); cur = cur.Add(slot) {
151			end := cur.Add(slot)
152			if end.After(close) {
153				end = close
154			}

156			for _, b := range booked {
157				if !b.Status.IsActive() {
158					continue
159				}
160				if !b.StartTime.Before(end) || !b.EndTime.After(cur) {
161					continue
162				}
163				active += b.PartySize
164			}
165			remaining := capacity - active
166			if remaining < 0 {

168			}

170				StartTime:       cur,
171				EndTime:         end,
172				Capacity:        capacity,
173				ActivePartySize: active,
174				RemainingSeats:  remaining,
175				Available:       remaining > 0,
176			})
177		}

############################
# Stage 1: build
############################
FROM golang:1.23-alpine AS builder

RUN apk add --no-cache git ca-certificates

WORKDIR /src

# Install templ generator first (pinned to module version)
RUN go install github.com/a-h/templ/cmd/templ@v0.2.778

# Copy source
COPY . .

# Generate templ Go files BEFORE resolving dependencies so the
# generated package is visible to `go mod tidy`.
RUN templ generate

# Resolve and download dependencies based on the full source tree
RUN go mod tidy

# Build a static binary
ENV CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux GOARCH=amd64
RUN go build -trimpath -ldflags="-s -w" -o /out/server ./cmd/server

############################
# Stage 2: runtime
############################
FROM alpine:3.20 AS runtime

RUN apk add --no-cache ca-certificates tzdata wget && \
    addgroup -S harbor && adduser -S harbor -G harbor

WORKDIR /app

COPY --from=builder /out/server /app/server
COPY --from=builder /src/migrations /app/migrations
COPY --from=builder /src/seed /app/seed

# Create writable mount points so docker volumes inherit harbor ownership.
RUN mkdir -p /app/keys /backups /exchange && \
    chown -R harbor:harbor /app /backups /exchange

USER harbor

EXPOSE 8080

ENTRYPOINT ["/app/server"]

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Single-command HarborWorks test runner.
#
# Brings up the test stack (postgres + a one-shot tests container that hosts
# the cover-instrumented binary), runs the unit_tests/ and API_tests/ suites
# inside the container, and propagates its exit code.
#
# Exit codes mirror the inner runner:
#   0 — all tests passed and coverage thresholds met
#   1 — at least one test failed
#   2 — coverage shortfall

set -uo pipefail

cd "$(dirname "$0")"

PROJECT="harborworks-test"
COMPOSE_FILE="docker-compose.test.yml"

cleanup() {
  echo
  echo "── Tearing down test stack ───────────────────────────────────────"
  docker compose -f "$COMPOSE_FILE" -p "$PROJECT" down -v --remove-orphans >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
}
trap cleanup EXIT

echo "── Building test image ───────────────────────────────────────────"
docker compose -f "$COMPOSE_FILE" -p "$PROJECT" build tests
BUILD_RC=$?
if [ $BUILD_RC -ne 0 ]; then
  echo "build failed"
  exit $BUILD_RC
fi

echo
echo "── Running test suites ───────────────────────────────────────────"
# Allow callers to sweep thresholds: UNIT_THRESHOLD=80 API_THRESHOLD=80 ./run_tests.sh
# Compose env_file/inline merging means we have to write a small override file
# so thresholds pass through cleanly; alternative is `-e` on `run`.
export UNIT_THRESHOLD="${UNIT_THRESHOLD:-90.0}"
export API_THRESHOLD="${API_THRESHOLD:-90.0}"

# `up --abort-on-container-exit --exit-code-from tests` propagates the tests
# container's exit code reliably across compose versions (unlike `run`).
docker compose -f "$COMPOSE_FILE" -p "$PROJECT" up \
  --abort-on-container-exit \
  --exit-code-from tests \
  --no-color
RC=$?

echo
echo "── Done (exit ${RC}) ─────────────────────────────────────────────"
exit $RC
